title: leucanthemum bauhini
intro: Leucanthemum bauhini, also known as the Shasta Daisy, is a perennial flowering plant native to North America, characterized by its white or pink daisy-like flowers and yellow centers. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, due to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones and flavonoids. The health benefits of Leucanthemum bauhini include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve skin conditions, and exhibit antimicrobial properties, making it useful in treating various ailments. Leucanthemum bauhini can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures for medicinal use, and its flowers and leaves can also be used in topical applications for wound healing and skin care.
